response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software engineer, but the profile diverges sharply from this embedded security/networking role. The most important blockers are hard requirements that are simply absent: native-level Japanese (the candidate lists only English, Romanian, and German) and strong C for UNIX development (only ~6 months of C/C++ at National Instruments years ago, with the recent career centered on TypeScript/Node/DLang web and microservice work). There is no evidence of Linux kernel source modification, L2/L3 switching, or security technologies like MACsec or 802.1X. Despite a solid engineering background, the gap against the core qualifications is too large to justify an interview.
strengths: Broad, long software-engineering track record across web, backend, and some embedded/robotics work
Some C/C++ experience (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI; C++ wave-field-synthesis thesis on Linux)
Strong testing/CI/CD and software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, trunk-based development)
concerns: No native-level Japanese — a hard requirement that is unmet
No demonstrated C/UNIX, Linux kernel modification, or embedded networking security (MACsec, 802.1X) experience
No Layer-2/Layer-3 or network-switch experience; recent work is web/cloud/TypeScript-focused

key_factors.factor: Lacks C/UNIX, Linux kernel, and embedded security skills
